# Overview

## What this deck covers

Two layers sit between a cleaned ping stream and a routed trajectory:

- **Valhalla** — an open-source routing engine built on OSM data. It ships the road graph as fixed-size geographic tiles and matches our pings to it.
- **Trucktrack partitioning** — how we shard trips on disk so the work above is cheap to load and parallelize.

The rest of the deck walks each layer with maps of real Ontario data.

## Why tiles

Loading the entire North American road graph for every short truck trip is wasteful: a 10 km local delivery only needs the streets within a few kilometres of its pings. Valhalla solves this by:

- Cutting the world into fixed lat/lon tiles
- Storing each tile as a self-contained `.gph` file
- Loading only the tiles a request actually touches, with an LRU cache on top

A tile bundle for one province (Ontario, ~1400 tiles) is small enough to ship inside a container.

## What trucktrack adds on top

Valhalla decides *which roads exist*. Trucktrack decides *which trips live next to which on disk* so that batches of related trips land in the same files and warm the same Valhalla tiles:

- **Tier** — bucket trips by spatial extent (local / regional / longhaul)
- **Tile** — pick a Valhalla-style tile for the trip's anchor point
- **Hilbert order** — sort trips inside a tile so spatially close trips stay close in the file
